summary: KnowER myth/fact classification: 5 statements about regional economic trends classified as myth/fact/partial_truth/unverified with confidence ratings and source quality

myth_fact_data.topic: Regional economic growth myths and facts

items.statement: The region has lost 40% of its manufacturing jobs since 2005
items.explanation: Provided data shows a 28% decline in manufacturing employment, not 40%. The 40% figure appears to conflate job losses with facility closures. The actual decline is significant but less severe than claimed.
unknowns: Current-year data not yet available in provided input
Part-time to full-time conversion rates unclear

items.statement: The county offers no tax incentives for new businesses
items.explanation: Provided county economic development documents list three active tax incentive programs: PILOT (Payment In Lieu of Taxes), downtown revitalization exemption, and small business startup credit. The claim directly contradicts available primary documentation.

items.statement: Population has been declining for the last decade
items.explanation: Provided census estimates show a consistent 0.3-0.5% annual population decline over the documented period. While the exact count varies by estimation method, the downward trend is confirmed across multiple data points.
unknowns: Full decade data not available in input — only 7 years covered

items.statement: The regional university is expanding its engineering program by 50%
items.explanation: No documentation in provided input supports or refutes this claim. The university's program expansion plans are not mentioned in any of the source materials. Cannot classify without additional information.
unknowns: No university planning documents in provided input

items.statement: All major employers in the region are downsizing
items.explanation: Provided employer data shows 3 of 8 major employers grew headcount, 2 remained stable, and 3 reduced. 'All major employers downsizing' is a false overgeneralization of a mixed picture.
unknowns: Some employer data is 12+ months old

myth_fact_data.operator_brief: Of 5 statements reviewed: 1 fact (population decline), 2 myths (no tax incentives, all employers downsizing), 1 partial truth (job loss percentage exaggerated), 1 unverified (university expansion). Key verification priorities: obtain current-year employment data, verify university expansion plans, confirm full-decade population figures. No external action needed — analysis based solely on provided input.

audit_trail.methodology_notes: Classification based on provided input text only. No external action taken.

next_steps_recommendation: Route myth/fact results to operator for review. No external action needed.
